Defendant Courthouse Ventures, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiff In Intervention'S Deposition Subpoenas For Business Records And For A Protecitve Order
Off Calendar Sep-27-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jak Marquez, Esq. of Dowling & Marquez LLP, T:415.977.0444, ext.232 for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

Defendant's counsel stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT IN INTERVENTION COURTHOUSE VENTURES, INC.'s Motion To Quash Plaintiff In Intervention's Deposition Subpoenas For Business Records And For A Protecitve Order, as follows:

The motion for protective order is DENIED subject to the requirement that any production of records must be treated as confidential under an appropriate confidentiality stipulation or order. Esther Eames LLC articulates sufficient grounds to seek the records of CHV's transactions between MOAB and third parties relating to MOAB-owned properties to discover whether there have been improper overpayments. The financial records appear "directly relevant" to the claims and defenses related to these allegations. Look v. Penovatz (2019) 24 Cal.App.5th 61, 73 (party seeking discovery of an individual's personal financial information must show the information sought is directly relevant to a cause of action or defense such that disclosure is essential to fair resolution of the lawsuit).

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Imposition Of Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Counsel
Off Calendar Jul-23-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF MOAB INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Imposition Of Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Counsel.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion to compel is off calendar without prejudice to refiling within 45 days from appearance of plaintiff's substitute counsel. The moving party is a corporation required to be represented to prosecute a claim. See: Paradise v. Nowlin, 86 Cal. App. 2d 897 (1948). Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Jun-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT COURTHOUSE VENTURES, INC., MAXIMO INVESTMENTS, LLC, MOHAMMED REZAIAN, ZAHRA REZAIAN, FAEZEH REZAIAN, MOHAMMED HADIPOUR's Motion For Protective Order.

The motion for protective order is DENIED. Plaintiff served discovery requests by its attorney of record, Mr. Sperandio. From the docket it appears that the Court appointed a receiver for Plaintiff, though the powers of that receiver are the subject of other motions. It also appears Mr. Sperandio has moved to be permitted to withdraw. I decline any invitation to prejudge the motion to withdraw, the motions about the receiver and his powers, or other relief the Court may impose to resolve the conflict among the partners. See Jarvis v. Jarvis (2019), 33 Cal. App. 5th 113. Limiting the analysis to the motion for protective order under CCP 2017.020 and 2031.060 (the bases cited in the notice), Defendants fail to support the motion with anything more than generalized objections about undue burden. This is insufficient. The requests for sanctions are denied.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 directly by email to the Judge Pro Tem at sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
